title: [Bug]: Security label scalar fields implicitly coerce non-string values
summary: Security label scalar fields such as classification and retention_category accept non-string JSON values and silently coerce them to text.
observed: normalize_security_label_profile accepts values like {'classification': 123} and stores the classification as '123'.
expected: Security label scalar fields should require strings so policy metadata is explicit and reviewable.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects non-string security label scalar fields to raise ValidationError.
impact: Implicit coercion can hide publisher mistakes and weaken security-label vocabulary enforcement.
delivery_semantics: Invalid security label metadata follows existing validation failure handling. ACK ordering is unchanged.
security: The fix should fail closed for malformed security labels and avoid logging the label content.
acceptance: Security label scalar fields reject non-string non-null values.
String scalar values and omitted values remain valid.
The focused regression test proves numeric scalar fields fail closed.
The release notes mention stricter security label typing.
tests: Run focused security label tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and test report.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
